description: Customer-facing polish & admin tools — move link editors to /admin page, fix sitewide copy (labels, stamps, week page), share page last-updated + empty state, resume link on hire-me, home page copy rewrite, PM skills section on portfolio

userStories.title: Sitewide copy polish
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want every label, badge, and status text to feel like a finished product so nothing looks like a rough draft.
acceptanceCriteria: In `components/Sidebar.tsx` line 67, change the visitor branch of the `isOwner` conditional from `'MY PROGRESS'` to `'SAM'S PROGRESS'`
In `app/(main)/portfolio/page.tsx`, change the stamp text from `'✓ Logged'` to `'✓ Complete'`

In `app/(main)/week/[week]/page.tsx`, replace the line `

📦 Portfolio deliverable: {week.portfolioDeliverable}

` with `

Portfolio deliverable · {week.portfolioDeliverable}

`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create /admin page with link editors
userStories.description: As the site owner, I want a single private page where I can manage all 10 portfolio links so the public portfolio page stays clean and visitor-focused.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `app/(main)/admin/page.tsx` as a client component (`'use client'`)
Import `useProgress` from `@/contexts/ProgressContext` and `curriculum` from `@/data/curriculum`
Copy the `LinkEditor` component inline at the top of the file (same implementation as in portfolio/page.tsx: controlled input, Save button appears when dirty, calls `onSave(val)` on click)
If `!isOwner`, render: heading `'Admin'`, and a paragraph `'This page is only accessible to the site owner.'` with a link back to `/`

If `isOwner`, render: a heading `'Link manager'` in `font-display font-bold text-2xl`, a subtitle `'Paste a public link for each deliverable. Links appear on the portfolio and share pages.'` in `text-ink/70 mb-8`

Below the subtitle, render `curriculum.map((week) => ...)` — each item shows: week number label in `font-mono text-xs text-terracotta uppercase tracking-wider`, deliverable title in `font-display font-semibold`, and a `<LinkEditor>` that calls `setLink(week.week, url)` on save
Each week item is separated by a border-b border-line and has py-5 padding
Page is accessible at `/admin`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Remove link editor from portfolio page; add admin sidebar link
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want the portfolio page to be fully read-only with no embedded admin tools; as the owner I want quick access to the admin page from the sidebar.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/(main)/portfolio/page.tsx`, remove the `isOwner ? <LinkEditor> : link ? <a> : null` block from completed cards

Replace it with a single non-conditional block: if `link` is set, render an `<a href={link}>` styled `inline-flex items-center gap-1 mt-3 bg-forest text-paper font-mono text-xs px-3 py-1.5 rounded hover:bg-forest/90` with text `'View deliverable ↗'`; if no link, render nothing
Remove the `LinkEditor` function definition from `portfolio/page.tsx` since it is no longer used there

In `components/Sidebar.tsx`, add a `'Manage links →'` link pointing to `/admin` that renders only when `isOwner` is true — place it after the existing nav items, styled `block font-mono text-xs text-ink/50 hover:text-forest py-1 transition-colors`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Share page polish — last active date and empty state
userStories.description: As a recruiter visiting the share page, I want to see when this portfolio was last updated and a clear message if no deliverables are published yet.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/(main)/share/page.tsx`, after the `pace` variable, compute `lastUpdated`: get all values of `progress.completedTasks` (they are ISO date strings), find the most recent one with `new Date(Math.max(...dates.map(d => new Date(d).getTime())))`, format as `'Last active: June 14, 2026'` using `new Intl.DateTimeFormat('en-US', { month: 'long', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric' }).format(date)`; if `completedTasks` has no entries, set `lastUpdated` to `null`

Below the pace line (after the progress bar section), render `{lastUpdated &&

{lastUpdated}

}` — add it inside the `mb-10` progress bar div, after the existing pace line
In the deliverables list, for rows where `!done && !link`, change the label from `'In progress'` to `'Coming soon'` styled `font-mono text-xs text-ink/40`

If `linkedCount === 0`, render a callout above the deliverables list: `

Deliverables publish here as each weekly sprint completes. Check back or follow progress above.

`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Resume/CV link on hire-me page
userStories.description: As a recruiter, I want a direct link to Sam's resume so I can review it without asking.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/(main)/hire-me/page.tsx`, read `const resumeUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_RESUME_URL ?? ''` at the top of the component (it is a server component so env vars are available directly)
In the CTA button group (the `flex flex-wrap gap-3 mt-6` div), add a third button after the existing two: `{resumeUrl && Download resume}`
If `NEXT_PUBLIC_RESUME_URL` is not set or is an empty string, the button does not render
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Rewrite home page 'How to manage' section
userStories.description: As the site owner, I want the home page 'How to manage the series' section to feel like polished product copy instead of rough internal notes.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/(main)/page.tsx`, change the section heading from `{isOwner ? 'How to manage the series' : 'How to explore Growth in Practice'}` to the single string `'How it works'` for both owner and visitor (remove the conditional on the heading)

Rewrite the owner bullet list (the `isOwner` branch inside the `<ul>`) to three polished lines: (1) `'Work through one to two days per session at your own pace — lessons build progressively within each week.'` (2) `'Check off tasks as you complete them. Progress syncs across devices and updates the public view automatically.'` (3) `'Each week ends with a portfolio deliverable. Paste the link in Manage links to publish it on your portfolio page.'` — wrap `'Manage links'` in a `<Link href='/admin' className='text-forest underline'>` tag
Visitor bullet list remains unchanged
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: PM skills section on portfolio page
userStories.description: As a recruiter, I want a concise breakdown of PM skill areas covered so I can quickly assess relevance without reading each week's detail.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/(main)/portfolio/page.tsx`, add a new section after the upcoming cards grid (before the closing `` of the page wrapper)
Section has a flex header row: heading `'Skills developed'` in `font-display font-semibold text-lg` and a span `'across 10 weeks'` in `font-mono text-xs text-ink/50 ml-3`
Below the header, render a `grid grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3 gap-3 mt-4` with 6 hardcoded skill cards, each `border border-line rounded-lg p-4 bg-card`
Skill card content: skill name in `font-display font-semibold text-sm` and description in `text-xs text-ink/60 mt-1 leading-relaxed`

The 6 skills: (1) 'Consumer Growth' / 'Funnel analysis, activation, retention, and conversion frameworks' (2) 'User Research' / 'Discovery, synthesis, and translating insight into product decisions' (3) 'Product Strategy' / 'Roadmap prioritisation, opportunity sizing, and stakeholder alignment' (4) 'Experimentation' / 'Hypothesis design, A/B testing, and reading results without bias' (5) 'AI-Native Products' / 'Evaluation, trust, activation, and metrics for AI-powered features' (6) 'Product Communication' / 'PRDs, specs, influence without authority, and executive narratives'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
